A dental crown is sometimes called a “cap” because its purpose is to cover the entire portion of a tooth that is visible above the gumline. We may recommend a crown for your child if: A tooth is badly decayed or misshapen. A tooth has recently undergone endodontic treatment, such as pulp therapy. A tooth was damaged in an accident.
